raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 1.0,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": "",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The open-ended request invites feedback on the clarity and framing of the core contribution claim.",
    "R1.2": "The request requires an assessment of the narrative argument's flow from problem to solution to validation.",
    "R1.3": "The request implies checking whether the reported numerical results convincingly support the claims of practical speedups.",
    "R1.4": "Evaluating the claimed novelty ('first sketched Krylov method with...') is the most critical part of responding to 'What do you think?'.",
    "R1.5": "The request includes evaluating the overall narrative arc and paragraph-level organization of the abstract.",
    "R1.6": "Feedback on sentence-level clarity, conciseness, and style is a natural component of a general review.",
    "R1.7": "The request entails judging whether the work is correctly positioned against the cited prior art.",
    "RX.1": "The assessment must be explicitly conditioned on the limited information available in an abstract.",
    "RX.2": "The response must avoid making definitive judgments about the work's validity or impact.",
    "RX.3": "The agent must confine its feedback to an analysis of the provided text, not the underlying research itself.",
    "RX.4": "The feedback must be constructive and aimed at strengthening the presentation of the research.",
    "RX.5": "All points of feedback must be grounded in specific statements or claims made within the artifact."
  }
}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites feedback on the clarity and framing of the core contribution claim.
rationale.R1.2: The request requires an assessment of the narrative argument's flow from problem to solution to validation.
rationale.R1.3: The request implies checking whether the reported numerical results convincingly support the claims of practical speedups.
rationale.R1.4: Evaluating the claimed novelty ('first sketched Krylov method with...') is the most critical part of responding to 'What do you think?'.
rationale.R1.5: The request includes evaluating the overall narrative arc and paragraph-level organization of the abstract.
rationale.R1.6: Feedback on sentence-level clarity, conciseness, and style is a natural component of a general review.
rationale.R1.7: The request entails judging whether the work is correctly positioned against the cited prior art.
rationale.RX.1: The assessment must be explicitly conditioned on the limited information available in an abstract.
rationale.RX.2: The response must avoid making definitive judgments about the work's validity or impact.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must confine its feedback to an analysis of the provided text, not the underlying research itself.
rationale.RX.4: The feedback must be constructive and aimed at strengthening the presentation of the research.
rationale.RX.5: All points of feedback must be grounded in specific statements or claims made within the artifact.
